title: 1.1: Tests that the silences list filters to show only matching silences when a matcher expression is typed in the search bar
1.2: Tests that matcher fields are rendered when URL contains matcher query parameters
1.3: Tests that the equals operator is correctly selected in the form when a matcher with equals operator is in URL
1.4: Tests that the parsed equals operator matcher from URL populates the form input fields
1.5: Tests that the not-equals operator is correctly selected in the form when a matcher with not-equals operator is in URL
1.6: Tests that the parsed not-equals operator matcher from URL populates the form input fields
1.7: Tests that the regex operator is correctly selected in the form when a matcher with regex operator is in URL
1.8: Tests that the parsed regex operator matcher from URL populates the form input fields
1.9: Tests that the not-regex operator is correctly selected in the form when a matcher with not-regex operator is in URL
1.10: Tests that the parsed not-regex operator matcher from URL populates the form input fields
1.11: Tests that the alert rule field displays the rule title when editing an existing silence linked to an alert rule
1.12: Tests that the alert rule field displays the rule title when creating a new silence with alert rule UID in URL matcher
2.1: Places the test for filtering silences in the alerting unified test directory
2.2: Places the test for URL parameter pre-filling in the alerting unified test directory
2.3: Places the test for editing silence with alert rule in the alerting unified test directory
2.4: Places the test for new silence with alert rule UID matcher in the alerting unified test directory
3.1: All tests use Jest framework with describe/it blocks
3.2: All tests use React Testing Library utilities for rendering and querying
3.3: All tests use async/await patterns for asynchronous operations
4.1: Follows the pattern of using a render helper for rendering the component with routing context for silence tests
